transcript: Hi, my name is Jong, co-founder of Trepa. Our app rewards you for precise numerical predictions. The idea is simple: get closer, win bigger. Okay, sorry. Uh, prediction markets today think binary. Yes or no, up or down. But the real world runs on numbers, and binary bets kill precision. Imagine a prediction market: Will the highest temperature during Breakpoint be less than 25 degrees? If the actual outcome is exactly 25, Toly, who guessed 24, loses, but Lily, who guessed 31, wins, even though she was much farther away from the truth. So essentially, Toly is punished for being accurate, but directionally wrong. Trepa flips that. The closer you are to the real outcome, the higher your ROI, exponentially. Instead of betting yes or no, you are predicting an actual number: prices, earnings, macro, election margins, sports stats, you name it. And it's super simple. Simply slide to your number, stake your conviction, and win cash and clout if you're in the top 50% of closest predictions. And we launched on Solana four days ago, and we have run a fun little experiment during Breakpoint where we asked 50 attendees to predict the exact number of M&Ms in this jar. And today I can reveal that the exact number is 979, and the best predictor said 975. But more interestingly, the distribution of estimates took the shape of a beautiful bell curve around the actual outcome. And over time, we believe that the data that we are generating is valuable and high resolution, as well as complementary and, uh, upstream to prediction market signals. We are two co-founders from Germany who met on the Polymarket subreddit. Leon is a full-stack engineer who has built trading alerts for prediction markets, and I predicted the effect of monetary policies. And we are proud to be backed by some of the best in the space. So try out Trepa and thanks for listening.

# Base–Solana Bridge Goes Live with Chainlink CCIP, Unlocking Massive Cross-Chain Liquidity
![Liam Turner](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%2048%2048%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)
By[Liam Turner](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/authors/liam/)
December 6, 2025
* 3min read
![base-solana](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/wp-content/uploads/base-solana.jpeg)
**Key Takeaways:**
* The Base–Solana Bridge is now live on mainnet, secured jointly by Chainlink CCIP and Coinbase.
* Users and developers can transfer SOL and SPL tokens to Base apps, enabling unified liquidity across both ecosystems.
* The launch marks one of the most significant cross-chain expansions for Base, strengthening its position as a hub for multichain Web3 activity.
The long-anticipated Base–Solana Bridge has officially[launched](https://x.com/chainlink/status/1996657107597160887?s=20), creating one of the most consequentia

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates

Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

tags.impact_reason: Demo day pitch for a prediction market app, unlikely to have immediate significant impact on the Solana ecosystem.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a scientific glass jar containing a bell curve of glowing spheres as the semantic anchor. This directly visualizes the core concept from the transcript: the M&M prediction experiment where guesses formed a 'beautiful bell curve' around the true number. It represents the shift from chaotic gambling to high-resolution, precise data. For the homepage text, I integrated '979'—the actual number of M&Ms—onto a metal plaque at the jar's base. This acts as the 'ground truth' that the predictions are measured against, making the text diegetic and reinforcing the story's theme of accuracy.
visual_idea.description: A dramatic 1:1 macro shot looking down into the glass jar. The frame is filled with the dense cluster of glowing orbs at the peak of the bell curve. The focus is on the intense concentration of light and the beautiful, granular pattern of the individual data points, creating an abstract texture of luminous energy. The full shape of the jar is not visible.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A dramatic 1:1 macro shot looking down into a meticulously rendered laboratory glass jar. The entire square frame is filled with a dense, symmetrical cluster of glowing orbs, concentrated intensely at the peak of a bell curve, creating a central focus of luminous energy. The focus is acutely on the vibrant, granular pattern of the individual data points, which form an abstract, mesmerizing texture of light. The full shape of the glass jar is intentionally cropped and not visible, emphasizing the internal glow. Volumetric light beams pierce through the scene, highlighting the glass's precise refractions and the orbs' intense, neon glow. The atmosphere is high-contrast, with subtle global illumination revealing the nuanced details of the glass and the complex spatial arrangement of the data points. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, ambient occlusion, tight macro, --ar 1:1

editorial.headline: Trepa Launches Scalar Prediction Markets to Fix Binary "Yes/No" Inefficiency
editorial.standfirst: The new protocol rewards precise forecasting over binary outcomes, generating high-resolution data that transforms prediction markets from gambling venues into granular sentiment engines.
key_takeaways: Live on Solana Mainnet: Launched early December 2025, emerging from the Superteam and Colosseum ecosystem.
Scalar Mechanism: Replaces binary betting with exponential ROI based on proximity to actual values (e.g., exact CPI prints).
Data Alpha: Generates bell-curve sentiment distributions, offering institutions "upstream" signals before binary markets settle.
editorial.article_body_markdown: While 2025 has been the year of the prediction market, the sector suffers from a fundamental data problem: low resolution. Dominant platforms like Polymarket force complex reality into binary "Yes/No" tranches. If a user predicts a temperature of 24°C and the outcome is 25°C, they lose everything to a counterparty who guessed 31°C, simply because the binary threshold was set at 25°C. This binary trap punishes accuracy and rewards directionally correct but imprecise speculation. Trepa, which launched on Solana Mainnet days before Breakpoint, addresses this by introducing scalar markets. Instead of betting on a threshold, users slide to a specific number—be it exact earnings per share, macro inflation data, or election margins. Payouts are determined by an exponential curve based on proximity to the truth. In a live demonstration at Breakpoint involving an M&M jar count, the market consensus (975) landed within a 0.4% margin of error of the actual count (979), forming a perfect bell curve of crowd sentiment. For institutional investors, the alpha here is not the betting yield, but the data exhaust. Binary markets only reveal the *probability* of an event occurring; Trepa reveals the *distribution* of market expectations. This generates high-fidelity, upstream signals—effectively a crowdsourced probability density function for any metric. A macro hedge fund, for instance, could use Trepa’s "bell curve" data to price derivatives far more accurately than by using simple binary odds. Co-founded by Jong-Chan Chung and his partner Leon (who met on the Polymarket subreddit), Trepa represents a shift from "gambling" to "forecasting." By leveraging Solana’s low-latency infrastructure, the protocol offers a consumer-grade "slider" interface that abstracts the complexity of the underlying order book, positioning itself to capture the next wave of sophisticated retail and institutional forecasters.
